differentialBuckets.label: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: Diagnosis commonly requires 3 or more: waist >102 cm men or >88 cm women in ATP III US cut points, TG >=150, HDL <40 men/<50 women, BP >=130/85 or treatment, fasting glucose >=100 or treatment. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.

safetyChecks.action: Check and document as baseline bedside data for this workup. Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
safetyChecks.rationale: Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Metabolic Syndrome.

safetyChecks.management_change: Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
safetyChecks.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.

requiredExam.label: Inspect neck for acanthosis

source.source_section: Metabolic Syndrome curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate insulin resistance or cardiometabolic risk phenotype. Acanthosis supports insulin resistance and prompts metabolic risk counseling.
requiredExam.rationale: Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Metabolic Syndrome.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect the named area directly, comparing sides when relevant and documenting presence, absence, severity, and inability to assess.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When insulin resistance or metabolic syndrome is suspected
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: insulin resistance or cardiometabolic risk phenotype
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Acanthosis supports insulin resistance and prompts metabolic risk counseling.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Metabolic Syndrome, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.label: Assess lower leg edema

source.source_section: Metabolic Syndrome curated endocrine workup: conditional bedside maneuver add-ons
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

conditionalExam.action: Add when edema, dyspnea, heart failure, kidney disease, albuminuria is present; this evaluates volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication. Edema changes cardiometabolic complication framing, medication safety, renal/cardiac evaluation, and follow-up urgency.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Metabolic Syndrome.
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect and press over both lower legs or dependent areas for pitting, symmetry, tenderness, and proximal extent.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When dyspnea, kidney disease, heart failure, medication effect, or volume overload symptoms are present
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Edema changes cardiometabolic complication framing, medication safety, renal/cardiac evaluation, and follow-up urgency.
conditionalExam.limitations: Assess lower leg edema is supportive for Metabolic Syndrome, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect skin for xanthomas or xanthelasma

source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect skin for xanthomas or xanthelasma is supportive for Metabolic Syndrome, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.
